IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: Please note that this shutdown was previously scheduled for the 20th May 2026 but was cancelled on the day. Some trees have grown too close to overhead power lines in your area. If left unmanaged, branches may come into contact with the lines, which could lead to unexpected interruptions to your electricity supply. To help prevent this, a tree surgeon will cut back the trees. The work has been requested by a third party, and their appointed tree surgeon will carry it out. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut back trees near the overhead power lines. Doing this helps reduce the risk of you experiencing unexpected power cuts in the future and maintains a safe and reliable electricity supply as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Folder Lane. Multi-way traffic lights will be in place to allow the works to be carried out safely.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 300 metres across 2 sections of the network. Due to the scale of the work, the power interruption is approximately 5 hours. Completing this work in one visit will help reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Boxhill Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly inspect, maintain and upgrade the electrical equipment in your area. As part of this programme, we will be upgrading 15 electricity poles. These poles play an important role in supporting the overhead electricity network and helping us deliver a safe and reliable supply to homes and businesses. This work will also reduce the likelihood of unexpected power cuts in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. We understand that power interruptions are inconvenient and we will do everything we can to keep disruption to a minimum. During this time, you will be connected to a generator to provide you with electricity whilst we carry out the works. To connect and disconnect the generator we will need to switch off your power temporarily. This work is taking place near Millers Green Road and Shellow Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly inspect, maintain and upgrade the electrical equipment in your area.
As part of this programme, we will be upgrading 5 electricity poles and a piece of equipment called a transformer. We will also be carrying out some tree trimming where necessary. This equipment plays an important role in supporting the overhead electricity network and helping us deliver a safe and reliable supply to homes and businesses. This work will also reduce the likelihood of unexpected power cuts in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. To carry out this work safely, we need to temporarily switch off your electricity while we work on the electricity poles, as it is not safe to do so with live electricity flowing through them. This work is taking place near Button Street.

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by David at 09:55

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 09:59 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks. Today's power cut was an unexpected overhead network fault that affected 1570 properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. I'm pleased to report our staff have resolved this by diverting power to get your lights back on as soon as possible. Were your text messages delayed during this fault? Find out why here https://UKPN.short.gy/no-sms I personally apologise for the disruption this caused you. We aim to provide a 10/10 service in keeping you updated, so if you'd like to talk to us about anything or are still without power, please call us on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Donny at 10:06 - Our engineers have restored power to all 1570 properties in the Canterbury CT4 5 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around the overhead network.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: Due to a faulty piece of high voltage network equipment we've had to turn off your power.

Logs.Comment:
Our engineers are awaiting the arrival of our generator.

Although this will be temporary, engineers will still need to repair the issue before it can be removed and network power is resumed. Depending on the fault, it may take several days for our engineers to pinpoint the problem and plan in the work. The temporary generator will remain on site to provide power until we can locate the issue and restore the network back to normal running conditions.

If you'd like any further information, our team are always here to help. Sent by Lisa at 15:24.

Hi there. We've just had a shift change, so Theo, Will and Lisa are now here to keep you updated with progress from our field teams.

Our engineers have investigated and there's going to be a lot of work required to repair the issue safely, so we have decided to use temporary generators to provide power. We're still estimating we'll have power back on between 18:00 and 19:00

We're sorry to change the time frame and will let you know when they arrive.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: An underground electricity cable has faulted on our high voltage network, causing an area wide power cut.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Lisa at 15:20 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground network fault that initially interrupted power to 522 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 392 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 18:00 and 19:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find.
